description_long: This is a tall, amphora-style terracotta vase with a classic elongated ovoid body and a wide, flared rim. The vase features two prominent, curved handles that extend from the neck to the shoulder, providing both decorative and functional elements. Its surface is textured with natural-looking pitting and subtle color variations, giving it an aged, weathered appearance reminiscent of ancient Greek or Roman pottery. The base is slightly raised and circular, offering stability for upright placement. The coloration is predominantly a warm, earthy orange-brown, typical of fired clay, with lighter and darker patches that enhance its rustic charm. This vase would be well-suited for outdoor urban settings such as parks, plazas, or garden courtyards, serving as a decorative focal point.
description: A tall, amphora-style terracotta vase with curved handles, a flared rim, and a weathered, textured surface in earthy orange-brown tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing both curved handles and the wide, flared rim, with the textured body and stable base clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the vase, with one handle visible and the elongated neck and body shape emphasized.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying both handles and the full curvature of the vase's body and rim.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, showing the other handle and the vase's continuous textured surface.
